title: The route line on Glass map tiles is wrong
description: The route line drawn on map tiles sent to the Glass does not match the actual route, nor is it aligned to the streets/paths displayed in the tile.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Root cause: OsmAndSnippetRenderer.writeTurnGpx synthesized a fake 3-point diagonal (±0.00045° lat, ±0.00072° lon) instead of using the real route polyline, so the line on each snippet was a fixed diagonal through the turn — unrelated to the route and not aligned with any street.

Fix: render() now takes (turns, track); for each turn it slices the real route polyline ±150 m (WINDOW_M) of cumulative distance around the nearest track point and writes those points to the GPX. Empty-track / single-point fallback skips the snippet (returns empty PNG) rather than emitting a misleading shape. RideService.pushRoute updated to pass route.track. New unit tests cover the slicing logic (window size, start/end clipping, empty track, right-angle bend).

title: Glass hard-reboots (kernel panic) on tile update during navigation
description:
User reports: switching map color scheme mid-navigation, then reaching a turn where a new tile is sent, hard-crashes the Glass (full reboot, not app close).

INVESTIGATION (session 2026-05-30):
- Glass = OMAP4430 / PowerVR SGX540, kernel 3.4.94, Android 4.4.4.
- /proc/last_kmsg at investigation time showed a KERNEL PANIC: 'kernel BUG at hrtimer.c:1270' in __run_hrtimer <- hrtimer_interrupt <- twd_handler, Process swapper/0 (idle task). 'Kernel panic - not syncing: Fatal exception in interrupt'.
- BUT that trace is NOT the nav crash: last app (.osmglass.glass) activity at kernel ts ~92953s, last GPU (PVR) power-cycle ~90466s, panic at ~97366s — i.e. device was IDLE ~73min with no GPU activity before panicking. Spontaneous idle-time kernel timer flake, content-independent.
- Confirms the 'full reboot' is a kernel-level fault (consistent with symptom) but does not capture the real nav crash. last_kmsg only holds the most recent boot (~2.8h ring).
- Day/night fix (glass-nav-lsl) is NOT implicated: it only flips a nightMode boolean in phone-side render; cannot cause a Glass kernel panic.
- onSettingsChanged() (re-renders+re-pushes all snippets) is wired only to the glass plugin settings fragment, NOT OsmAnd's global map-scheme switch. Tiles are pre-rendered at route-prep time.

NEXT: live capture set up — self-restarting logcat to /tmp/glass_logcat_live.txt + reboot watcher pulling /proc/last_kmsg on crash. Awaiting user repro ride to capture the REAL nav-crash kernel trace + app-side correlation.

title: Merge phone-app into OsmAnd fork as GlassNavPlugin
description: Eliminate the phone-app/OsmAnd context switch by merging phone-app's responsibilities (BLE transport, packet streaming, snippet rendering, display settings, ride orchestration) into the OsmAnd fork as an in-process OsmandPlugin called GlassNavPlugin. Auto-start streaming whenever OsmAnd starts navigation, if a Glass MAC is paired. Plan file: /home/node/.claude/plans/having-to-manually-switch-twinkling-barto.md

End-to-end smoke test passed on real device with Glass headset.

title: Fix OsmAnd fork: align getActiveRoute and buildNavigationProgress on the raw RouteDirectionInfo list
description:
Implement the fix for glass-nav-lms. Both fork-added AIDL surfaces must agree on a single list of RouteDirectionInfo so currentTurnIndex from progress is a valid index into the turns from getActiveRoute.

Plan:
1. Add a package-private accessor on RouteCalculationResult to expose the raw 'directions' list (or a getter like getOriginalRouteDirections()). The aggregated cacheAgreggatedDirections list is fine for in-app UI but unsuitable for AIDL clients because it (a) constructs fresh RouteDirectionInfo objects and (b) drops skip-to-speak turns + shrinks as currentDirectionInfo advances.
2. In OsmandAidlApi.getActiveRoute, switch from rh.getRouteDirections() to the raw directions list. Ensure routePointOffset on each raw RouteDirectionInfo still maps cleanly into the polyline (it does — directions and locations share the same offsets, which is how the current code resolves t.setLat/setLon).
3. In OsmandAidlApi.buildNavigationProgress, replace dirs.indexOf(ndi.directionInfo) with ndi.directionInfoInd (already populated by RouteCalculationResult.getNextRouteDirectionInfo at line 1239: 'info.directionInfoInd = nextInd'). Drop the dirs lookup entirely.
4. Build the fork APK via the documented recipe (memory: osmand-glassnav-build-recipe) and install on the test device.

Acceptance:
- On the test device with simulated navigation running, log RideService 'PROGRESS' lines should show currentTurnIndex stepping 0 → 1 → 2 → ... as OsmAnd advances through directions (not stuck at lastIndex).
- Glass live card snippet + instruction text + TTS approach/imminent cues match the current OsmAnd step.
- distanceToNextTurnM corresponds to the displayed instruction (i.e. shrinks to 0 then jumps to the next turn's full distance).

Out of scope: aggregated-vs-raw turn UX tradeoffs (we deliberately want every raw turn on Glass; the fork's voice router still uses its own internal list for TTS in OsmAnd itself).
notes:
Patch applied across three files:
- OsmAnd/OsmAnd/src/net/osmand/plus/routing/RouteCalculationResult.java: added public getOriginalRouteDirections() returning the raw (unmodifiable) directions list.
- OsmAnd/OsmAnd/src/net/osmand/plus/routing/NextDirectionInfo.java: added public getDirectionInfoInd() (the field is package-protected, and OsmandAidlApi lives in net.osmand.aidl).
- OsmAnd/OsmAnd/src/net/osmand/aidl/OsmandAidlApi.java: getActiveRoute now uses route.getOriginalRouteDirections() instead of rh.getRouteDirections() (the aggregated cache); buildNavigationProgress sets currentTurnIndex = ndi.getDirectionInfoInd() — dropping the broken indexOf lookup against the aggregated list with fresh-minted objects.

Fork APK rebuilt successfully (BUILD SUCCESSFUL, 36s, ~1.16 GB). Remaining: on-device verification per acceptance criteria (install patched APK; confirm PROGRESS log shows currentTurnIndex stepping through turns, Glass snippet/instruction/TTS track the current OsmAnd step, distanceToNextTurnM corresponds to the displayed instruction).
close_reason: Verified on-device after parent bug closure.

title: Bug: Glass shows/speaks only the destination bundle — currentTurnIndex from fork is always -1
description:
On the OsmAnd-fork progress path, Glass displays only the destination's snippet and instruction; spoken directions are unrelated to the current OsmAnd-simulated position. Symptoms reported:
- Only destination map tile shown on Glass
- Only one instruction shown (does not match OsmAnd's current step)
- Distance updates, but appears unconnected to the trip
- Glass speaks directions that don't track OsmAnd's voice

Root cause (verified by source read):
OsmandAidlApi.buildNavigationProgress (OsmAnd/OsmAnd/src/net/osmand/aidl/OsmandAidlApi.java:2291) computes:
    List<RouteDirectionInfo> dirs = rh.getRoute().getRouteDirections(app);
    p.setCurrentTurnIndex(dirs.indexOf(ndi.directionInfo));
But RouteCalculationResult.getRouteDirections(app) (OsmAnd/OsmAnd/src/net/osmand/plus/routing/RouteCalculationResult.java:1283) returns 'cacheAgreggatedDirections' — a list of FRESHLY CONSTRUCTED RouteDirectionInfo objects (line 1297: 'p = new RouteDirectionInfo(...)') that aggregate over skip-to-speak turns. Meanwhile ndi.directionInfo comes from the RAW directions list (RouteCalculationResult.getNextRouteDirectionInfo at line 1228: 'info.directionInfo = directions.get(nextInd)'). RouteDirectionInfo has no equals() override, so indexOf returns -1.

Default ANavigationProgress.currentTurnIndex is -1 (ANavigationProgress.java:19). Phone-app RideService.streamProgressFromFork (phone-app/src/main/java/dev/glass/phone/ride/RideService.kt:364) then falls back:
    val turnIdx = if (p.currentTurnIndex in route.turns.indices) p.currentTurnIndex
                  else route.turns.lastIndex.coerceAtLeast(0)
So every Packet.Progress carries turnIdx = destination. Glass PacketDispatcher caches TurnBundles by turnIndex and looks up tb = destination bundle, then displays its PNG + instructionText and uses tb.kind for TTS — explaining all four symptoms.

Same inconsistency affects route export: getActiveRoute (OsmandAidlApi.java:2181) builds turns from rh.getRouteDirections() (aggregated, omits skip-to-speak turns), while progress's directionInfoInd indexes into raw 'directions'. The two index spaces don't agree even after the indexOf bug is fixed.
close_reason: Verified on-device: Glass snippet/instruction/TTS now track the current OsmAnd step, distance corresponds to the displayed instruction.

title: Option C design: AIDL surface, parcelables, versioning, fork applicationId + build pipeline
description:
Gates all Option C sub-tasks (glass-nav-u1y).

User decisions already locked in:
- Strategy: upstream PR with fork as fallback. Design AIDL additions to be upstream-acceptable.
- The patched OsmAnd installs alongside official OsmAnd on test devices.

Deliverable: short design doc in docs/ (or extended writeup in spikes/) covering:

1. New AIDL methods + parcelables to add to net.osmand.aidlapi.* — specifically:
   - Route export call: signature, parcelable shape (polyline points + per-turn list with street name, instruction text, distanceFromStartM, turn type int, lat/lon).
   - Periodic progress callback: registration call + callback parcelable (speedKmh, remainingDistanceM, etaSec, distanceToNextTurnM, next-turn type, next-turn street name).
   - Optional: reroute event callback.
2. Versioning: how the client detects a fork vs stock OsmAnd (e.g. interface version method, capability ping, or feature-detect via try/catch on the new method). Default-fall-back behaviour required by the avi/c7v acceptance criteria.
3. Fork applicationId / package strategy: pick a suffix (proposal: net.osmand.glassnav) and document required source changes. Add to OsmAndAidlClient.KNOWN_PACKAGES probe order + AndroidManifest.xml <queries>.
4. Build pipeline: how to build the patched OsmAnd APK from the sibling checkout and install on a test device. Note any signing / Google Play Services prerequisites.
5. Upstream-PR plan: which OsmAnd repos/branches to target, code style, naming conventions to match.

Acceptance: doc reviewed; fork-impl sub-tasks have unambiguous signatures + filenames to edit.
notes: Design doc landed at docs/option-c-aidl-design.md. Locks in: 3 new AIDL methods (getActiveRoute, registerForNavigationProgress, registerForRerouteEvents), 6 new parcelables in net.osmand.aidlapi.navigation, feature-detect versioning (no version method needed — fall back to BRouter/RouteMatcher when fork not present), fork applicationId net.osmand.glassnav as a new product flavor in OsmAnd/OsmAnd/build.gradle. Sub-task→section map at end of doc.
close_reason: Design doc at docs/option-c-aidl-design.md covers all five required sections (AIDL surface, parcelables, versioning, fork applicationId + manifest, build pipeline, upstream-PR plan) with concrete filenames/signatures that unblock glass-nav-nzt, glass-nav-ei1, glass-nav-45y, glass-nav-p9i, glass-nav-avi, glass-nav-c7v.

title: Position is often lost during a ride
description:
The phone app often stops updating the position during a ride for several minutes at a time. Other navigation apps continually update the position under similar circumstances.

Update the app to more aggressively maintain position tracking. It is acceptable to increase battery usage if necessary.
notes:
Root cause: RealGpsSource only subscribed to LocationManager.GPS_PROVIDER, so when raw GPS dropped (urban canyon, tunnel, indoors) the fix stream stalled for minutes with no fallback.

Fix: rewrote RealGpsSource to register listeners on FUSED (API 31+), GPS, NETWORK, and PASSIVE providers simultaneously. Incoming Locations are merged with the canonical Android isBetterLocation heuristic so lower-quality fixes only override the current best when meaningfully fresher or comparable accuracy. Also seeds the flow with the freshest last-known fix on subscribe so consumers don't sit at null while providers warm up. Manifest already grants COARSE + FINE, so NETWORK_PROVIDER works without new permissions.

Verified: ./gradlew :phone-app:compileDebugKotlin (BUILD SUCCESSFUL).
close_reason: Multi-provider RealGpsSource (FUSED/GPS/NETWORK/PASSIVE) prevents stalls when raw GPS drops

title: Route does not always recalculate following deviation
description: Upon deviating from the planned route, I expect a recalculation in no more than 30 seconds, preferably less. The app does not do so reliably.
close_reason: Replaced brittle 2-consecutive off-route counter with a 7-of-last-10-fixes rolling window (RouteMatcher.kt) so GPS jitter / parallel-road flicker no longer resets detection. Reduced REROUTE_COOLDOWN_MS from 30s to 5s (RideService.kt) so a BRouter failure can't blow past the 30s budget. Added tests for trigger, flicker tolerance, and clearing.

title: Changes to route do not update Glass quickly
description:
I stopped my route on the phone app and selected a new one, and it took about one minute to get a new instruction on my Glass.

A new route or recalculation should immediately push to the Glass.
close_reason: Stop button now keeps RideService + BT transport alive; selecting a new route reuses the existing connection via RideService.startRoute() instead of paying for a Bluetooth reconnect. Also tracks currentRouteId so stopRide() can send a matching RouteEnd(CANCELLED) to Glass.

title: Glass app disconnects during use
description:
During a ride, sometimes the Glass app disconnects from the phone app even though the two are still Bluetooth paired. It does not reconnect, and it is not clear to the user that there is a problem.

Ideally, the app would never disconnect. Investiate and fix sources of connection instability.

IF the Glass app does lose its connection, it should try to reestablish the connection. If it cannot, it should wake up the screen and show a "DISCONNECTED" message in large text until dismissed.

It is acceptable to modify both apps to resolve this issue.
close_reason: Added application-level Keepalive (5s ping, 15s watchdog) to RFCOMM + TCP transports on both sides; closes the socket on silent BT drops so the existing reconnect path runs. On Glass, transport disconnect now triggers a 10s-grace screen-wake alert with large 'DISCONNECTED' text on the LiveCard, cleared on reconnect. 6 new Keepalive unit tests + existing TcpTransport roundtrip pass.

title: Improve text contrast
description:
Detect the current light/dark setting and use a dark text color with light halo when light mode is in use, and a light color with dark shadow when dark mode is in use.

The current colors are fine in dark mode.
close_reason: Implemented light/dark text contrast. Detection: average luminance of a sparse 16x16 pixel grid of the snippet bitmap (computed once per turn in decodeCached, since the phone renders the PNG in its own day/night theme — no protocol change needed). Threshold mid-grey -> dispLightMode. Rendering: each corner now has paired dark/light TextView variants in livecard_nav.xml (dark = light text #FFFFFF/#FFD580 + black halo, unchanged; light = dark text #000000/#663300 + white halo); renderViews.applyCorner toggles visibility per mode. RemoteViews can't recolor a blur shadow at runtime (setShadowLayer isn't @RemotableViewMethod), so the contrast flip is a visibility toggle between two pre-styled views (user-chosen approach). glass-app:assembleDebug passes.

title: Add arrival time to text slots
description: The text slots in the plugin settings can include turn instruction, distance to turn, etc.... Add arrival time.
close_reason: Added ARRIVAL_TIME display field. New Packet.DisplayConfig.Field.ARRIVAL_TIME (between ETA and SPEED); Glass PacketDispatcher.renderField computes wall-clock arrival = now + etaSec, formatted in device 12/24h preference (distinct from ETA's remaining-duration). No Progress wire change — etaSec already present. OsmAnd plugin: fieldLabelRes case + glass_nav_field_arrival_time string ('Arrival time'); settings/Controller pick it up via Field.values(). Codec unchanged (ordinal-based); CodecTest sweeps values() so it's covered.

title: Replace top/bottom slots with corners
description: The plugin settings let the user specify what to put in the top and bottom text slots. Replace these two slots with four corners.
close_reason: Replaced top/bottom display slots with four configurable corners (top-left/right, bottom-left/right). Protocol: DisplayConfig now carries 4 corner Fields + new NONE field; Codec encodes 4 bytes + muteTts. Glass: PacketDispatcher renders 4 corners, NavLiveCardService/livecard_nav.xml lay out 4 corner TextViews + a centered status overlay (Done/Rerouting/DISCONNECTED) via new showStatus(). Plugin: 4 corner prefs + settings UI rows + strings; controller pushes all 4.

title: Glass app crashes (BadTokenException) when tapping live-card menu
description:
Tapping the Glass touchpad to open the live-card menu crashes the Glass app with:
  java.lang.RuntimeException: Unable to start activity ...MenuActivity: android.view.WindowManager$BadTokenException: Unable to add window -- token null is not valid; is your activity running?
  at MenuActivity.onCreate(MenuActivity.java:21) -> openOptionsMenu()

Root cause: MenuActivity.onCreate() calls openOptionsMenu() before the activity window is attached (no window token yet). Glass GDK pattern is to open the options menu from onAttachedToWindow(), not onCreate().

Fix: move openOptionsMenu() from onCreate() to an onAttachedToWindow() override.

Impact: app process dies (FATAL EXCEPTION main), but Android restarts the process + NavLiveCardService (observed pid 1351 -> 5532, service back up). Likely drops the active RFCOMM streaming session until phone reconnects. NOT a device reboot (distinct from glass-nav-yuf kernel panic). Glass app was NOT modified today; pre-existing. Observed during glass-nav-lsl theme-update test 2026-05-30.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: FIXED + VERIFIED on-device (2026-05-30). Moved openOptionsMenu() from MenuActivity.onCreate() to an onAttachedToWindow() override. Rebuilt glass-app debug, reinstalled to Glass (needed uninstall+reinstall: installed build had INCONSISTENT_CERTIFICATES vs this machine's debug key; Glass app persists no data — only empty cache + lib symlink — so no loss). MenuActivity now Displays cleanly (+290ms/+625ms) with no BadTokenException/FATAL across multiple launches; app process stays alive. Not committed (per user).

title: Glass map tiles should match light/dark mode on phone
description: On the phone, OsmAnd may be permanently set to light or dark mode, or it may switch automatically. Sometimes dark mode on the phone results in dark tiles being sent to the Glass, and sometimes it does not. They should always match.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Fixed: Glass map snippets were rendered with ThemeUsageContext.APP (app UI theme only), while the phone's actual map uses ThemeUsageContext.MAP (honors DAYNIGHT_MODE: DAY/NIGHT/AUTO/SENSOR). That's why dark tiles reached Glass only when the map's day/night happened to be tied to the app theme.

Fix: MapBitmapDrawer.initAndDraw() now calls an overridable protected isNightMode() (default = APP, preserving upstream track-import/point-editor/AIDL thumbnail behavior). The Glass-only FixedTileBoxTrackDrawer (in OsmAndSnippetRenderer.kt) overrides it to use ThemeUsageContext.MAP, so Glass tiles always match the on-screen map theme.

Files:
- OsmAnd/OsmAnd/src/net/osmand/plus/myplaces/tracks/MapBitmapDrawer.java
- OsmAnd/OsmAnd/src/net/osmand/plus/plugins/glassnav/render/OsmAndSnippetRenderer.kt

Verified: :OsmAnd:compileAndroidFullLegacyArm64Debug{Kotlin,JavaWithJavac} BUILD SUCCESSFUL.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Reopened: the render-context fix (APP->MAP) only makes tiles match AT ROUTE-PREP TIME. User found switching map theme mid-ride doesn't update the cached Glass tiles. Added live mid-ride matching in GlassNavController.kt:
- StateChangedListener on DAYNIGHT_MODE (strong-ref field; pref listeners are weak-ref'd).
- prewarmNightMode added to prewarm cache key so a day/night change invalidates bitmaps + forces re-render.
- onMapThemeChanged(): debounced 400ms, no-ops when effective MAP night mode unchanged, re-pushes current-turn-first (sendOrder). Safeguards limit the tile burst (glass-nav-yuf crash suspect).
- stopStreaming cancels themeChangeJob + resets prewarmNightMode.
Builds clean (arm64 debug Kotlin+Java). NOT yet installed on phone — install is a supervised test because the re-push burst may reproduce the hard reboot.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
VERIFIED on-device (Pixel 4a + Glass, 2026-05-30). Installed glassnavOpengldebug build. Mid-ride day/night toggle now live-updates Glass tiles:
- Toggle to night: phone logged 'map theme changed (night=true) — re-rendering + re-pushing snippets' (01:04:04); 24 bundles re-pushed current-turn-first (queued order 17..23,0..16); Glass received #0-#23; tile byte sizes differed from pre-toggle set (re-render confirmed, not a cached re-send).
- Toggle back to day: 'map theme changed (night=false)' (01:05:29), re-pushed again.
- No app crash, no device reboot across multiple full 24-tile bursts. A full burst is survivable — weakens the 'tile burst causes kernel panic' theory for glass-nav-yuf.
Debounce + no-op-when-unchanged + current-turn-first safeguards all behaved as designed.

title: Gradle build fails on non-ASCII test resource filename (collectTestResources)
description:
Any full Gradle build fails in the :OsmAnd-java:collectTestResources task with:

  Cannot access input property 'rootSpec$1$1' of task ':OsmAnd-java:collectTestResources'. ... Failed to create MD5 hash for file '/home/zak/code/glass-nav/resources/test-resources/search/ludwigstraße.json' as it does not exist.

The file resources/test-resources/search/ludwigstraße.json has a non-ASCII character (ß) in its name; Gradle's input-snapshotting can't resolve/hash it in this environment (likely a filesystem encoding / JVM file.encoding mismatch), so the task aborts before compilation.

Impact: blocks any full build; currently must be worked around with '-x :OsmAnd-java:collectTestResources -x :OsmAnd-java:test' to compile.

Possible fixes: (1) set JVM file.encoding/LANG to UTF-8 for the Gradle daemon; (2) mark the task untracked via Task.doNotTrackState(); (3) rename/ASCII-escape the test resource. Investigate which is appropriate.

Discovered while verifying glass-nav-t2t.
close_reason: Added self-enforcing UTF-8 locale guard to OsmAnd/gradlew (user's fork). Root cause was POSIX locale -> sun.jnu.encoding=ASCII breaking non-ASCII filename access; -D flags and doNotTrackState proven ineffective, only a real locale env var works. Verified: build succeeds under empty-LANG with no manual locale.

title: The first turn isn't shown on Glass
description: In most simulated trips, the map tile for the first turn is not shown on the Glass. The voice instruction is read, unless voice instructions are muted.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Root cause: GlassNavController.onRoutingDataUpdate computed turnChanged = (lastTurnIndex != -1 && turnIdx != lastTurnIndex). lastTurnIndex resets to -1 at route start, so the first turn never fired a Packet.TurnAlert. TurnAlert is what surfaces the Glass LiveCard + wakes the screen (PacketDispatcher.onPacket -> service.onApproachingTurn -> liveCard.navigate). So the first turn's snippet only appeared once the rider crossed the distance approach threshold, while voice (maybeSpeakInitialDirection) played at route start regardless -> 'voice read but map not shown'.

Fix: dropped the 'lastTurnIndex != -1' guard so the first segment also fires a TurnAlert, matching turns 2..N. Code-only; not yet verified on device (needs OsmAnd build + simulated trip).

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
First fix (first-turn TurnAlert) was necessary but insufficient — user confirmed still broken after rebuild/reinstall, plus a new symptom: on a 2nd trip without restarting glass-app, several turns pass before map tiles appear.

Real root cause: GlassNavController.pushSnippets called renderer.render(turns) which renders EVERY turn's snippet sequentially (each up to RENDER_TIMEOUT_MS=8s) and only enqueued the TurnBundles AFTER the whole batch finished. So turn 0's bitmap couldn't ship until the last turn rendered -> rider passed the first (and on longer/colder routes several) turns before any tile reached Glass. Glass side resets cleanly per RouteStart, consistent with the symptom being producer-side regardless of glass-app restart.

Fix: added OsmAndSnippetRenderer.renderEach(onSnippet callback) that emits each Snippet the instant it renders, in route order; pushSnippets now enqueues each TurnBundle immediately so turn 0 ships first (within one render instead of after N). Kept the first-turn TurnAlert change (surfaces/wakes the card for the first leg). Code-only; not yet verified on device.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Added tile/bitmap pre-warming during route planning (per user request). prepareRouteState now calls prewarmSnippets() which starts the snippet render the moment a route is calculated — startStreaming fires on newRouteIsCalculated, which OsmAnd raises during route preview (before following mode), so rendering overlaps the rider's route-review window and warms OsmAnd's map tiles before navigation begins. Each turn's render result lands in a per-turn CompletableDeferred (prewarmSlots, keyed by routeId+orientation); pushSnippets (on transport connect) awaits these in order, so a turn rendered during planning ships instantly and the transport never blocks on a cold render. Falls back to re-render on routeId/orientation mismatch (e.g. orientation changed via settings). stopStreaming cancels the prewarm job and clears slots. Decouples render start from BT-connect time, which was the remaining cold-tile gap after the per-turn streaming fix. Code-only; awaiting on-device confirmation.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
User report after prewarm: first turn still blank AND Glass spoke the initial direction during route preview, before navigation started. Root cause: startStreaming fired on newRouteIsCalculated, which OsmAnd raises during route PREVIEW — so RouteStart+TurnBundles (hence the spoken initial direction + card) were pushed before the rider tapped Go.

Fix: split prepare/prewarm from publish. startStreaming now only prepareRouteState()+prewarmSnippets() at calc time (renders + warms tiles during the preview/review window). Actual transport open + route push is deferred to maybeStartPublishing(), gated on RoutingHelper.isFollowingMode. Since OsmAnd has no 'navigation started' callback, onRoutingDataUpdate polls isFollowingMode and opens the transport on the first location tick after Go. Mid-nav reroute still republishes over the live link. Net: tiles/bitmaps pre-rendered during preview, but no voice/card until navigation actually starts. Code-only; awaiting on-device confirmation.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Still blank first turn after publish-gating. Consistent 'first turn only' (not random) => structural: turn 0 is rendered first and pays OsmAnd map-renderer cold-start. Two possible mechanisms: (A) render times out -> EMPTY png (0B) -> Glass skips image; (B) render returns a non-empty but blank-map bitmap (renderer not warm). Added retry-on-empty in OsmAndSnippetRenderer.renderEach (MAX_RENDER_RETRIES=2, RETRY_BACKOFF_MS=1000) which fixes (A): a turn that comes back empty is re-rendered after a short backoff once the engine has warmed. Need logcat to confirm A vs B: producer line 'queued TURN_BUNDLE #0 (KIND, <N>B)' — 0B=>A (retry should now fix), >0B=>B (needs renderer-readiness gate instead). Code-only; awaiting on-device log.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
LOGCAT REVEALED THE ACTUAL CAUSE (my earlier empty-png/cold-start guesses were wrong): TURN_BUNDLE #0 = 205829 bytes (~206KB), and every Glass PROGRESS referenced turnIndex #2+ (never #0/#1). So bundles are huge AND transmit in index order, so the two ~206KB bundles the rider never uses (#0,#1) hog the slow RFCOMM link before bundle #2 (the rider's actual first turn) arrives -> first turn blank through its approach.

Two fixes: (A) OsmAndSnippetRenderer encodes lossy WEBP (quality 80) instead of PNG-100 -> ~5-10x smaller bundles; marker is composited on Glass post-decode so no quality impact; BitmapFactory auto-detects format (no Glass change). (B) pushSnippets sends in sendOrder(count, currentTurnIndex()) = current turn first, wrapping, so the approaching turn reaches Glass ahead of passed turns. Retry-on-empty render (added earlier) kept as defensive robustness. SEPARATE OBSERVATION worth a follow-up: turns #0/#1 are never a Progress target (first is #2) — possible spurious early turns from buildTurns; the spoken initial-direction uses #0/#1 so may be off. Code-only; awaiting on-device confirm.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Indexing dig findings: (1) FIXED off-by-one in Turn.distanceFromStartM — RouteDirectionInfo.distance is the leg AFTER the turn, but buildTurns added it to cumulative before setting distanceFromStartM, so every turn's distance-from-start was overstated by its own outgoing leg (visible in the spoken 'for N meters'). (2) Found contract mismatch behind the bad initial voice: Glass maybeSpeakInitialDirection expects index 0 = departure START bundle, but buildTurns skips the TurnType.C departure so index 0 is the first real turn — producer never sends a START bundle. (3) compactTurnIndex mapping is correct; first PROGRESS #2 is just the rider already past depart+turn0 (getNextRouteDirectionInfo returns currentDirectionInfo+1). Added diagnostics: buildTurns dumps the full turn list; buildProgress logs rawDirInd->turnIdx. Need an on-device run to dump the turn list and confirm the duplicate 'Turn left Maurienstraße' before deciding the START-bundle fix approach.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Implemented option B for the initial spoken cue. Protocol: RouteStart gained startLabel (additive trailing pstr, backward-compatible decode like muteTts/markers; 4-arg + legacy 3-arg ctors). Producer: prepareRouteState captures the skipped departure direction's getDescriptionRoute into startLabel; pushRoute sends it. Glass: PacketDispatcher stores routeStartLabel and now speaks the initial cue from the first Progress that resolves a cached bundle — keyed to the rider's current/approaching turn (which sendOrder ships first) rather than the old cache[0]+cache[1] (the leading turns now ship last). Cue: '<departure> for <distToTurn>m, then <current maneuver>'. Added CodecTest coverage (with-startLabel round trip + legacy-without-startLabel decode). :protocol:test green (BUILD SUCCESSFUL), :glass-app compiles. OsmAnd producer not buildable locally. Needs on-device check of the spoken initial direction.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: First on-device run showed ROUTE_START from='Head 38 m' — getDescriptionRoute on the departure returns 'Head <dist>' with NO street name (it just appends formatted distance to a pre-built descriptionRoute that lacks the name). Switched producer to capture departure.streetName (fallback .ref) instead, sent as a bare name. Glass now phrases it: street known -> 'Head on <name> for <dist> meters, then <maneuver>'; unnamed/empty -> 'In <dist> meters, <maneuver>'. Added producer diagnostic logging street/ref/desc. :glass-app compiles. Need next on-device run to confirm departure.streetName is populated (log line 'departure: street=...'); if it's also empty for this route the start road is genuinely unnamed and the 'In N meters, <turn>' fallback applies.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Confirmed on-device: departure street='Pfenningsbusch', startLabel='Pfenningsbusch'. Initial cue now resolves to 'Head on Pfenningsbusch for <dist> meters, then <first turn>'. getStreetName() is the reliable source (this run desc happened to include the name, 'Head Pfenningsbusch 230 m', but last run's desc was 'Head 38 m' without it). Option B initial-cue work is functionally complete. Remaining: optional cleanup of the investigation-only diagnostic logging (buildTurns turn-list dump @INFO/route, per-tick progress rawDirInd->turnIdx @DEBUG, departure @INFO/route).

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Added a 'Debug logging' plugin setting (per user request) instead of removing the investigation diagnostics. GlassNavSettings.debugLogging (boolean, default off, global+shared) + SwitchPreferenceCompat in GlassNavSettingsFragment; GlassNavController.debugLogging() now gates the buildTurns turn-list dump, the per-tick progress rawDirInd->turnIdx log, and the departure log. Off by default = quiet logcat; toggle on to capture diagnostics. Mirrors the existing ttsMuted pref pattern; OsmAnd module not buildable locally but changes are a direct copy of known-good patterns. Braces balanced across all 4 files + strings.

title: Directions and position to Glass are delayed
description:
During simulated navigation, the position shown on the Glass often lags significantly behind the position shown on the phone. Sometimes turn instructions are delayed as well.

Check for a common root cause and improve it if possible. If there are separate causes, address them all.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Root cause: GlassNavController's phone->Glass outbound path was unordered + unbounded.

1) onRoutingDataUpdate did scope.launch{t.send(progress)} on multi-threaded Dispatchers.IO. Each routing tick (several/sec under sped-up simulation) spawned a coroutine racing for the FrameWriter lock -> Progress packets written out of order and piled up unboundedly. (The class comment even claimed 'single dispatcher to keep ordering predictable' -- false, IO is a thread pool.)
2) pushSnippets writes N real 640x360 PNG TurnBundles over the SAME FrameWriter lock, so Progress blocked behind multi-second image transfers, then flushed as a stale burst -> marker lags then jumps.
3) No conflation: position is latest-wins but every stale tick was transmitted, so the marker fell progressively behind.

Fix: single ordered writer coroutine draining two channels (startWriter/stopWriter):
- controlChannel (UNLIMITED, reliable, select-priority): RouteStart/TurnBundle/TurnAlert/RouteEnd/DisplayConfig -- never dropped; bundles land before the Progress that depends on them.
- progressChannel (CONFLATED, latest-wins): per-tick Progress; stale ticks dropped while the link is busy.

This guarantees in-order delivery AND sheds stale positions, fixing both the position lag and the turn-instruction delays (shared path). Also covers a slow Glass consumer: socket backpressure blocks the writer and conflation drops the queued-behind stale ticks, so no Glass-side change was needed.

Verified: :OsmAnd:compileGlassnavOpengldebugFatDebugKotlin BUILD SUCCESSFUL. Not yet validated on hardware (RFCOMM is hardware-only; no container coverage).

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Reopened: phone-side conflation reduced but did not eliminate the lag. Residual cause is consumer-side. The phone's conflation only sheds when its socket write blocks; small Progress packets buffer in the RFCOMM/Glass read path, and Glass rendered EVERY buffered frame synchronously on the RFCOMM reader thread (decode 640x360 PNG -> ARGB copy -> drawPositionMarker -> RemoteViews setViews IPC). That per-frame cost let a backlog of stale frames trail the rider's real position.

Glass-side fix (NavLiveCardService):
- Dedicated 'LiveCardRender' HandlerThread. updateRemoteViews now stores the latest frame in a volatile pendingFrame and coalesces a single render via removeCallbacks+post. The RFCOMM reader thread returns immediately and drains the socket fast; only the freshest frame is ever painted, so stale buffered frames are skipped instead of rendered.
- Decode cache: a turn's PNG is the same byte[] instance across every Progress (PacketDispatcher's cached TurnBundle), so we decode once and reuse the base bitmap, copying only to stamp the moving marker -- cuts most per-frame cost.
- All views/setViews access (renderPendingFrame, onTransportConnected, showDisconnectAlert) is now serialized on the render thread, removing a pre-existing reader-vs-main race. showDisconnectAlert clears pendingFrame so a stale frame can't overwrite DISCONNECTED.

Could not compile glass-app in this environment (AGP 8.13.0 not in offline cache, network fetch hangs); verified by review. Needs on-device validation.

title: Map tiles on the Glass sometimes cut off the upcoming turn
description: In travel-direction-up orientation, sometimes map tiles are cropped so that segments of the path drawn on them are not in frame. This is acceptable when the cropped section is more than one minute away. It is not acceptable if the cropped section includes a turn that is less than 30 seconds away.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Root cause: in TRAVEL_UP, OsmAndSnippetRenderer rendered the source at rotatedSourceSize() (bbox of the rotated 640x360 dest) and let OsmAnd auto-fit the route's axis-aligned (north-up) lat/lon bbox into it. But only the inscribed rotated 640x360 rect is visible after the rotate+centre-crop. For intermediate bearings the axis-aligned fit over-zooms along the travel axis: at bearing 45 only ~216m of the 600m (+/-300m) window is visible (~108m each side ~= 26s at cycling speed), so a turn ~30s away lands in the cropped corners. Bearings 0/90 happened to fit, masking it.

Fix: SnippetBounds.buildTileBox now picks the largest integer zoom (7..17) at which the WHOLE window, run through the exact project() rotate+crop, stays inside the 640x360 destination (windowFitsDestination). OsmAndSnippetRenderer injects that same RotatedTileBox into the drawer via new FixedTileBoxTrackDrawer subclass (overrides createTileBox), so the bitmap and the marker projection use one box. North-up collapses to the old bbox-containment test (unchanged). Verified by simulation: all bearings now keep the full +/-300m window in frame.

title: Navigation does not start on Glass for a new destination
description: With the Glass app open, still showing the previous instruction, starting a new trip on the phone does not result in the Glass showing map tiles and turns from the new trip. When the Glass app is open, starting a new route on the phone should promptly begin showing tiles and instructions on the Glass.
close_reason: Closed

title: Position marker isn't very accurate on Glass
description: During simulated navigation, where the position is always precisely on-route on the phone, the Glass position marker often shows significant lateral deviation from the route.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Root cause found & fixed in render/SnippetBounds.kt.

PROBLEM: the Glass position marker is projected by SnippetBounds, which APPROXIMATED OsmAnd's snippet projection with a continuous meters-per-pixel fit of the GPX bbox times a fixed PADDING_FACTOR=1.4. But OsmAnd's TrackBitmapDrawer.createTileBox() fits the bbox using a DISCRETE integer zoom ladder (largest integer zoom 7..17 whose RotatedTileBox still contains the bbox). The continuous fit can disagree with the real discrete zoom by up to a full zoom level (~2x scale), so the marker was placed at the wrong scale relative to the drawn route = the observed lateral deviation. (The simulated position is exactly on-route, so this is purely a projection-scale error, not a GPS error.) The TRAVEL_UP path additionally rotated the projected point by +rotationDeg while the bitmap is actually rotated by -rotationDeg (rotateIntoDestination) — a sign error.

FIX: SnippetBounds now reconstructs the EXACT same RotatedTileBox createTileBox() builds (same centre, integer-zoom fit, density, pixel dims) and projects via tileBox.getPixXFromLatLon/getPixYFromLatLon — the identical call TrackBitmapDrawer uses to draw the track, so the marker lands pixel-exact on the route line. Stage 2 maps source->destination with the same centre-rotation(-rotationDeg)+centre-crop as rotateIntoDestination (identity for NORTH_UP). Removes PADDING_FACTOR / equirectangular approximation entirely and corrects the rotation sign. Public API (project/startLat/startLon/startBearingDeg/transformBearing/widthPx/heightPx) unchanged, so GlassNavController.computeMarker is untouched.

STATUS: implemented + reasoned through; NOT yet runtime-verified (full OsmAnd Android build not run here). Needs a simulated-nav test to confirm the marker tracks the route, esp. in TRAVEL_UP.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
CORRECTION after testing: my first pass also flipped the Stage-2 rotation sign in project() (used toRadians(-rotationDeg)), which regressed TRAVEL_UP — the marker swung around / changed heading unpredictably. The SnippetBounds.rotationDeg field is ALREADY the canvas rotation angle the renderer applies (rotatedTravelUp sets it to -startBearingDeg, the same value handed to canvas.rotate in rotateIntoDestination), so project() must rotate by +rotationDeg, not -rotationDeg. Reverted to +rotationDeg (matches the pre-existing, correct behaviour). NORTH_UP was unaffected (rotationDeg=0). transformBearing was already correct and untouched.

Net state: (1) scale fix via real RotatedTileBox retained; (2) rotation sign restored to the original correct value. Glass-side marker compositing (NavLiveCardService.drawPositionMarker) is centred on (px,py) with no anchor offset, so it is not the source of the previously-reported constant offset — that was the projection scale, now addressed by the tile box. Still needs runtime verification in both NORTH_UP and TRAVEL_UP.

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: VERIFIED by user: marker now tracks the route accurately in both NORTH_UP and TRAVEL_UP. Closing.

title: Glass app falls behind phone directions
description: In simulated navigation, directions shown on the Glass app can lag significantly behind what's shown on OsmGlass on the phone. This may be a result of using the phone's voice prompts as a trigger. Investigate options to keep the two close to synchronized, or to start pushing turns and map tiles to the Glass early to account for Bluetooth having limited bandwidth.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Root cause found & fixed in GlassNavController.kt (OsmAnd plugin).

PROBLEM: turn-index space mismatch, not voice-prompt triggering.
- buildTurns() compacts route.immutableAllDirections, SKIPPING non-maneuver directions (continue/C, off-route) and re-indexing kept turns 0..M-1. TurnBundles are keyed by this compacted index.
- buildProgress() used Progress.turnIndex = NextDirectionInfo.directionInfoInd, which indexes the FULL un-skipped directions list.
- The route's leading 'continue' start (+ any mid-route straight) is skipped, so the spaces diverge. Glass then resolves the wrong TurnBundle, or null near route end ('PROGRESS without cached TURN_BUNDLE'), freezing the live card on the last resolvable turn = the observed lag.

NOTE: bandwidth is NOT the cause — all TurnBundles are already pushed up front at RouteStart (pushSnippets), and snippet PNGs are currently empty stubs; Progress packets are tiny.

FIX: buildTurns() now returns rawToCompacted IntArray (raw direction index -> compacted maneuver index, skipped dirs map forward to next maneuver); buildProgress() translates via new compactTurnIndex(directionInfoInd). Wire protocol and Glass code unchanged.

STATUS: implemented + reasoned through; NOT yet runtime-verified (full OsmAnd Android build not run here). Needs a simulated-nav test to confirm Glass tracks the phone.

title: Zoom level and centering cut off upcoming turns
description: In some cases, the zoom level and centering results in the path for upcoming turns being cut off in the Glass app. Scaling and centering should be adjusted to reduce the frequency of this phenomenon.
close_reason: Renderer now sizes the source bitmap per-turn to the bbox of a rotated 640x360 destination (rotatedSourceSize), so OsmAnd auto-fits the polyline window to a frame matching what's actually visible. Replaces the fixed 736x736 source + center-crop that was losing ~50% of vertical content on tall routes - the cause of upcoming-turns being cut off, especially in TRAVEL_UP where the forward portion always dominates the vertical axis post-rotation. Build verified: compileGlassnavOpengldebugArmonlyDebugKotlin compiles clean (only pre-existing deprecation warnings).

title: Position arrow is not shown
description: Prior to integration into the OsmAnd fork, a position marker arrow was displayed in the Glass app. Now it isn't. It should be.
close_reason: Closed

comments.author: unknown
comments.text: Fix: GlassNavController.buildProgress was sending MARKER_NONE for marker x/y/bearing. Ported the projection from old phone-app RideService.kt — cache snippet bounds per turn during pushSnippets, project lastFixedLocation through bounds.project() (with travel-up rotation handled via bounds.transformBearing). Cleared on stopStreaming. Fallback to startLat/Lon when outside bitmap, same as original. Could not verify with Gradle build due to pre-existing UTF-8 filename issue in OsmAnd-java/test-resources (ludwigstraße.json fails Gradle MD5 hash).

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Identified additional cause beyond prior fix: snippetBoundsByTurn was set only AFTER pushSnippets finished rendering all bitmaps. Rendering is sequential with 8s per-turn timeout, so on a 10-turn route the marker pipeline was dormant for tens of seconds — long enough to look broken in practice.

Fix: split the bounds computation out of OsmAndSnippetRenderer.render() into a new computeBounds() that does the polyline math only (microseconds). GlassNavController.pushSnippets now populates snippetBoundsByTurn before kicking off render(), so the very next Progress packet projects a marker.

Verified build: LANG=C.utf8 LC_ALL=C.utf8 ./gradlew :OsmAnd:assembleGlassnavOpengldebugArmonlyDebug -x test → BUILD SUCCESSFUL. APK at OsmAnd/OsmAnd/build/outputs/apk/glassnavOpengldebugArmonly/debug/. Needs on-device verification.

In OsmAnd: set destination, start navigation. Verify within ~1s: RouteStart received, TurnBundles render, Progress streams at ~1Hz, distance/ETA/turn update on Glass. Toggle a display slot mid-ride → reflected within one Progress tick. Cancel nav → BLE socket closes, FGS notification disappears, Glass goes idle. adb logcat -s GlassNav:V for transport trace.

Add GlassNavRoutingListener implementing IRouteInformationListener + IRoutingDataUpdateListener. On newRouteIsCalculated(true,...) AND paired MAC present → controller.startStreaming() (connect transport, send RouteStart + TurnBundles built from rh.getRoute().getImmutableAllDirections()). On routeWasCancelled/Finished → stopStreaming. On onRoutingDataUpdate → emit Progress packet from RoutingHelper accessors. Derive TurnAlert timing from segment-result transitions. title: Spike: can OsmAnd expose computed route polyline + turn list to phone?
description:
Resolves the design blocker for the Option A migration (glass-nav-u1y).

OsmAnd's public AIDL navigate()/calculateRoute() returns only a boolean — neither hands back the computed route polyline or the upfront turn list. RideService today depends on having both:
- track (List<LatLng>) — needed by RouteMatcher for off-route detection + distance-from-start.
- turns (List<Turn>) — sent upfront as N×TURN_BUNDLE before Progress streaming starts.

Goal: pick one of three paths and document the answer so implementation tasks can proceed.
1. Probe getActiveGpx() after navigate() — does OsmAnd surface the active route as a selected GPX we can read back?
2. Hand-build turns + track incrementally from streaming ADirectionInfo (requires protocol change: TurnBundles sent on-the-fly, not upfront).
3. Keep BRouter solely as a routing source that returns GPX; OsmAnd only does turn streaming + snippets + voice. (Defeats some of the maintenance-burden goal but preserves the protocol shape.)

Output: a short writeup in spikes/<timestamp>/ choosing one path, with the AIDL evidence behind the choice. Update epic glass-nav-u1y notes with the choice.
close_reason: Answered: no public OsmAnd AIDL exposes computed route polyline or turn list. ASelectedGpxFile is metadata-only; ADirectionInfo carries only next-turn distance/type/leftSide; navigate()/calculateRoute() return boolean. Three paths evaluated in spikes/20260525-205327/bk3-summary.txt — Path 1 (getActiveGpx) dead, Path 2 (incremental ADirectionInfo) significantly degraded UX, Path 3 (keep BRouter for routing) viable. User chose Option C — fork OsmAnd to add a route-export AIDL — so the epic is re-scoped around extending the AIDL surface rather than working around its limits.

On scoped-storage Android, OsmAnd (running as net.osmand.plus) cannot read another app's app-scoped dir. CreateGpxBitmapParams takes either a File (path string, OsmAnd must open directly) or a Uri. Switch to FileProvider Uri with grantUriPermission to the bound OsmAnd package.

title: Map OsmAnd TurnType ints to protocol TurnKind enum
description:
ADirectionInfo.turnType is an int from OsmAnd's TurnType class. Build the translation table to our protocol.TurnKind enum (STRAIGHT, LEFT, RIGHT, SLIGHT_LEFT, SLIGHT_RIGHT, SHARP_LEFT, SHARP_RIGHT, U_TURN, ROUNDABOUT, ARRIVE, START, etc — confirm by reading protocol/TurnKind.java).

OsmAnd TurnType constants live in net.osmand.router.TurnType (in OsmAnd-java module). Produce a Kotlin extension fun TurnType.toTurnKind(): TurnKind. Roundabout exits need turnAngle handling — write down whether we surface exit number or fold into ROUNDABOUT generically.
close_reason: TurnTypeMapping.kt + unit test added under phone-app/.../osmand. Built and tested clean. Fidelity gaps documented in class kdoc: (1) roundabout exit number is dropped by OsmAnd V2 AIDL (getValue() strips exitOut) — folded to KL/KR; (2) ADirectionInfo.isLeftSide is never set by V2 AIDL impl — treat as meaningless; (3) C (continue) and OFFR map to null so caller can skip/handle as deviation; (4) TRU collapses into TU. Recommendation: future protocol bump should add STRAIGHT + ROUNDABOUT to TurnKind for full fidelity, but the current 11-entry enum is workable for the spike.

title: Spike: snippet quality from getBitmapForGpx vs current SnippetRenderer
description:
For 5 representative turn locations from a test route, generate a per-turn snippet two ways and visually compare:
A) Current phone-app SnippetRenderer (Mapsforge, zoom 18, ~640x360 PNG).
B) OsmAnd getBitmapForGpx fed a short GPX clipped to ±50 m around the turn, with size and density chosen to match.

Capture: readability of the road network, arrow overlay placement, label legibility, file size, render latency. If quality is acceptable, Option A is viable end-to-end. If not, escalate to Option C (add a getMapSnapshot AIDL method).
acceptance_criteria: Side-by-side PNGs for 5 turns and a recommendation: ship-with-AIDL, ship-with-overlay-tweaks, or escalate-to-Option-C.
notes:
Result from spike session 20260525-163731 after fixing FileProvider URI handoff (glass-nav-lkn).

Per-turn measurements (Brandenburg Gate → Reichstag):
  turn  osmAnd_ms  osmAnd_B   mapsforge_ms  mapsforge_B
  0     1077       182743     538           99003
  1     335        129810     164           80892
  2     273        132344     171           62017
  3     275        97166      111           44135
  4     303        80923      116           51974

Latency: first OsmAnd call pays a ~1.1s warmup (cold AIDL+renderer). Subsequent calls 273–335 ms — under the 500 ms threshold for pre-rendering at route-start. Mapsforge consistently ~2× faster but both fit the budget after warmup. Pre-warming with a throwaway call would amortize the cold cost.

Bytes: OsmAnd PNGs 80–183 KB vs Mapsforge 44–99 KB — roughly 2×, well under the 5× BT-throughput red line.

Visual (5 PNGs saved in spikes/20260525-163731/0ha-snippets/):
- OsmAnd: richer context — traffic lights, pedestrian crossings, bus stops, POI icons (restaurants, monuments), building polygons, multilingual labels. North-up. GPX overlay rendered as a thin line in the color we pass (Color.RED).
- Mapsforge: cleaner blue route line styled by our SnippetRenderer, less POI clutter, current familiar look.

Both are legible at Glass prism size. OsmAnd's GPX overlay is less prominent than Mapsforge's blue route — likely tunable via wider stroke / different color.

Recommendation: ship-with-AIDL with overlay-tweaks. getBitmapForGpx is viable for the Option A migration; no need to escalate to Option C. Migration work is mostly visual tuning (overlay width/color, optionally cold-start prewarm) rather than capability gaps.
close_reason: See notes for full analysis. 0ha: ship-with-AIDL + overlay tweaks; getBitmapForGpx viable, no Option C needed. 51h: Option A — pipe OsmAnd's params.played array straight to Glass TTS.

title: Spike: validate updateNavigationInfo cadence and content vs RideService.streamProgress
description:
Drive OsmAnd through a known test route (use OsmAnd's simulation feature or a recorded GPX) with our AIDL client attached. Record: callback frequency, jitter, whether distanceTo monotonically decreases, whether turnType updates atomically at the turn boundary, whether speed/remaining/ETA are accessible (via separate registerForUpdates + AInfo if not present in ADirectionInfo).

Deliverable: a short markdown note (in beads --notes, not a repo file) summarizing whether ADirectionInfo + companion AIDL calls together cover the Packet.Progress fields (distanceToTurnM, bearingDelta100, speedKmh, remainingDistanceM, etaSec). Flag any gaps that would force a fork (Option C).
acceptance_criteria: Note in issue covers: cadence (Hz), gap analysis vs Packet.Progress, recommended AIDL call combination, and a go/no-go for full migration.
notes:
Result from spike session 20260525-152059 (Brandenburg Gate → Reichstag, OsmAnd simulate-route, 44.7s, 15 events).

Cadence: avg_hz=0.34, min_gap=3ms, max_gap=7503ms, monotonicity_anomalies=0.
Pattern: events arrive in close pairs (3–5ms apart) every ~1.5s or ~3–4.5s, with one 7.5s gap. Roughly half are duplicate emissions of the same state.

Gap vs Packet.Progress requirements:
- distanceToTurnM: present, monotonically decreases within a turn segment.
- turnType: present, maps to TurnKind cleanly except turn_type_int=1 → <null> (CONTINUE/STRAIGHT, expected).
- isLeftSide: confirmed always false (known AIDL bug).
- speedKmh, remainingDistanceM, etaSec: NOT present in ADirectionInfo. Would need registerForUpdates + AInfo.

Go/no-go: ADirectionInfo alone is INSUFFICIENT to drive Packet.Progress at the current 1Hz cadence.
- avg_hz 0.34 << 1.0 target
- max_gap 7.5s >> 2.0s threshold; would visibly starve the Glass display
- Speed/remaining/ETA fields aren't carried by ADirectionInfo regardless

Recommended AIDL combination for Option A: registerForUpdates (for speed/remaining/eta + cadence backstop) + ADirectionInfo (for turnType/distanceToTurn). If that combo still can't hit 1Hz with bounded jitter, escalate to Option C (patch OsmAnd).

Caveat: 44.7s/15 events is a small sample. Gap structure is consistent enough that a longer run is unlikely to flip the conclusion. Follow-up validation of registerForUpdates cadence is folded into epic glass-nav-u1y.
close_reason: ADirectionInfo cadence insufficient (0.34Hz, 7.5s max gap) and missing speed/remaining/eta — Option A still viable but requires registerForUpdates alongside. registerForUpdates validation folded into glass-nav-u1y. Full analysis in notes.

title: Epic: Migrate phone-app to drive OsmAnd via AIDL (Option C — patched OsmAnd fork)
description:
Replace the hand-rolled BRouter+Mapsforge stack on the phone with a thin client that delegates routing, map data, and turn computation to OsmAnd via its public AIDL service (net.osmand.aidl.OsmandAidlServiceV2). Glass protocol and glass-app stay unchanged. Scope: validation spike + minimal migration of phone-app's RideService to consume OsmAnd events.

Goals:
- Drop maintenance of phone-side BRouter binding, Mapsforge offscreen renderer, RouteMatcher, RouteDataPrefetcher.
- Reuse OsmAnd's offline maps, multi-profile routing, voice router, and turn-detection.
- Keep RideService as the orchestrator that translates OsmAnd events → Packet.* on the Glass transport.

Open questions to be answered by sub-tasks before commit-level migration begins:
1. Is updateNavigationInfo(ADirectionInfo) cadence + content sufficient to drive Progress + TurnBundle?
2. Does getBitmapForGpx produce per-turn snippets of acceptable quality vs current SnippetRenderer output?
3. How does ADirectionInfo.turnType (TurnType int) map to our TurnKind enum?
4. Can onVoiceRouterNotify replace our maybeSpeakInitialDirection on Glass?

Out of scope: forking OsmAnd, adding new AIDL methods (that is Option C, a follow-up if A is insufficient).
notes:
Spike glass-nav-hva (closed 2026-05-25): ADirectionInfo alone is too slow (0.34 Hz, 7.5s max gap) and lacks speed/remaining/eta.
Spike glass-nav-bk3 (closed 2026-05-25): no public OsmAnd AIDL exposes the computed route polyline or upfront turn list — see spikes/20260525-205327/bk3-summary.txt.

Decision (2026-05-25): epic re-scoped from Option A → Option C. We will fork OsmAnd to add the missing AIDL surface, rather than work around its limits. The original Option A approach can't drop BRouter without either (a) regressing the protocol to incremental TURN_BUNDLEs and losing street names in turn instructions, or (b) keeping BRouter forever for routing.

Option C scope (new AIDL surface to add to fork):
1. Route export — after navigate()/navigateGpx()/calculateRoute(), an AIDL call that returns the active route's polyline + structured turn list (street name, instruction text, distance-from-start, turn type, lat/lon). This replaces BRouter + GpxTurnExtractor.
2. Progress callback — a periodic callback at ≥1 Hz with remainingDistanceM, speedKmh, etaSec, plus the next-turn fields ADirectionInfo already carries. This replaces phone-side RouteMatcher + speed buffer math.
3. (Optional) Reroute event — fire when OsmAnd internally recomputes the route after an off-route condition, so phone re-pulls #1.

Open questions to settle in the Option C design sub-task before implementation:
- Upstream PR vs permanent fork? Affects naming, code style, and how much we touch beyond the AIDL.
- Package coexistence: a patched OsmAnd should likely have its own applicationId (e.g. net.osmand.glassnav) so it can be installed alongside official OsmAnd on the same device.
- AIDL versioning: bump the interface version and detect old/new at client connect time.
close_reason: Closed

title: Plugin settings are not reflected on change

comments.author: unknown
comments.text:
Root cause: BaseSettingsFragment.registerPreference() reassigns every preference's OnPreferenceChangeListener to the fragment itself AFTER setupPreferences() runs (line 734), clobbering the per-preference change listeners GlassNavSettingsFragment attached. So setSummary()/notifyControllerSettingsChanged() never fired; the value still persisted via the OsmAnd PreferenceDataStore (correct after reopen) but the on-screen summary stayed stale and the controller wasn't notified. Same clobbering the existing click-listener comment already documented.

Fix: moved change handling into an onPreferenceChange() override (idiomatic OsmAnd pattern, cf. DevelopmentSettingsFragment) keyed by pref id; removed the four now-dead setOnPreferenceChangeListener blocks. Override writes the backing GlassNavSettings pref before notifying (data-store persist happens after the listener returns true) and updates the summary from newValue. Verified: compileGlassnavOpengldebugArmonlyDebugJavaWithJavac succeeds.

title: Investigate duplicate ANavigationProgress emits per location fix
description: During glass-nav-vzm on-device verification, the fork's nav progress stream emitted two identical ANavigationProgress frames ~4ms apart per location fix (observed at 12:47:32.780/.784 and 34.292/.298 with identical lat/lon/speed/remainingDistanceM). Only one progress subscription was active. Per memory osmand-fork-progress-callback-keys the IRoutingDataUpdateListener fires per location fix and is debounced via navProgressLastEmit, so duplicates should not be possible from a single registration. Check OsmandAidlApi.registerForNavigationProgress for double-registration (e.g. registering both IRoutingDataUpdateListener and some other hook) or for the debounce window being too short (< few ms). Low priority — phone-side throttles via streamProgress anyway, but the duplicate doubles AIDL IPC traffic.
notes: Root cause: OsmandAidlApi.registerForNavigationProgress installed a listener that broadcast to every callback in aidlCallbackListenerV2 with KEY_ON_NAVIGATION_PROGRESS bit set, not just its own. Any second live registration (e.g. transient overlap during off-route reroute, or DebugActivity + RideService concurrent) made each listener deliver to both callbacks, so a single client received 2x per location fix. Fix: per-id callback map (navProgressTargets) — each listener delivers only to its own IOsmAndAidlCallback. Touches OsmandAidlApi.java + OsmandAidlServiceV2.java. Builds clean.
close_reason: Switched OsmandAidlApi.registerForNavigationProgress to per-id callback delivery (navProgressTargets map) instead of broadcasting to all KEY_ON_NAVIGATION_PROGRESS subscribers. Eliminates the duplicate-emit class caused by sibling subscribers / transient overlap. glassnav APK builds clean.

title: On-device verification: getActiveRoute AIDL returns populated parcelable
description:
Follow-up to glass-nav-nzt (route-export AIDL). The fork's getActiveRoute() implementation in OsmandAidlApi.java:2166 + dispatcher in OsmandAidlServiceV2.java:1607 builds clean against the osmand-api wrapper, but the nzt acceptance criterion 'New AIDL method callable from a stub test app and returns a populated parcelable when an OsmAnd navigation session is active' was not executed (no Android device hooked up at implementation time).

Steps:
- Build glassnav APK: ./gradlew :OsmAnd:assembleGlassnavOpengldebugArmonlyDebug (in OsmAnd sibling checkout).
- adb install + cold-launch; trigger a navigation session inside the fork (any route).
- From a stub test client (or phone-app once glass-nav-p9i lands hasOptionCExtensions): bind to net.osmand.aidl.OsmandAidlServiceV2, call getActiveRoute(new AGetRouteParams()).
- Assert: return true; ARoute.polyline non-empty; ARoute.totalDistanceM > 0; ARoute.turns non-empty with sane turnType / instructionText / distanceFromStartM monotonically increasing.
- Also verify fingerprint matches what the parallel ARerouteEvent stream reports.

Acceptance: empirical confirmation the AIDL call returns the expected payload on a real device. Failure here would mean glass-nav-c7v (BRouter → OsmAnd routing replacement) cannot proceed.

Likely subsumed by glass-nav-p9i's bind verification once phone-side hasOptionCExtensions probe + KNOWN_PACKAGES land, but tracked separately so it doesn't get silently skipped.
notes: Empirical verification PASS on Pixel 4a-16 against net.osmand.glassnav with active ~14km route (Jacksonville FL). getActiveRoute() returned: polyline=420pts, totalDistanceM=13970, turns=14, fp=3. All acceptance criteria met: polyline non-empty, totalDistanceM>0, turns non-empty with valid turnType enums (1/2/5/6/8), meaningful instructionText, distanceFromStartM strictly monotonic (0,558,1722,4320,...,13970). hasOptionCExtensions()=true. Progress stream confirmed at 1Hz with nextTurnType/idx matching getActiveRoute turns array. Fingerprint match against ARerouteEvent NOT empirically tested (would require triggering a recalc); mechanism verified via code review per memory osmand-fork-progress-callback-keys. Verified via OsmAndAidlDebugActivity.kt (added per-turn dump + monotonicity assert + logcat output). Drove via adb input tap; captured via adb logcat -s OsmAndAidlDebug:I.
close_reason: On-device verification PASS. See notes for full empirical results.

title: Spike: replace initial-direction TTS with onVoiceRouterNotify messages
description:
Our Glass-side PacketDispatcher.maybeSpeakInitialDirection() composes '<start.instructionText> for <first.distanceFromStartM> meters, then <maneuver phrase>'. OsmAnd's voice router emits its own pre-formatted phrases via onVoiceRouterNotify(OnVoiceNavigationParams). Decide: (a) pass OsmAnd's phrases through as TTS payloads (new optional packet, or repurpose an existing field), or (b) keep our own composer and just use ADirectionInfo data.

Read OnVoiceNavigationParams shape, sample a route, and report which approach preserves the user-facing audio behavior with less code.
notes:
Decision based on spike session 20260525-160222 (the cleanest read with road context — later sessions used navigateGpx(snap=off) which produces degenerate "Continue for X meters" only and is not useful for assessing voice quality).

OsmAnd's onVoiceRouterNotify delivers natural, TTS-ready phrasing in the params.played array. Representative events captured during a Brandenburg → Reichstag route:
- "in 80 meters, turn left , then keep right"
- "turn right onto  Friedrich Ebert Platz"
- "Continue for 60 meters " then "and arrive at your destination "
- "You have reached your destination Reichstag"
- "The trip is 400 meters time is  6 minutes" (route start)

Phrasing is natural enough to TTS directly. Cosmetic warts (double spaces, trailing spaces) trivially fixable on receive.

Implementation notes for whoever picks up the migration:
1. The 'composed' JSON column in 51h-voice.jsonl is a buggy in-spike-tool concat that emits "[object Object]" for parcelable args. Do NOT use it. Read params.played and join with spaces.
2. Filter out commands[0]=="off_route" events — those are recoverable conditions, not driving prompts.
3. Filter "route_new_calc" / "route_recalc" route-summary events at start unless we want trip-distance announcements on Glass.
4. Units follow OsmAnd's user preference (we saw both metric and US units across sessions); decide whether to override per-glass-user or accept OsmAnd's setting.

Recommendation: Option A — repurpose the protocol to carry pre-composed strings from params.played. Drop PacketDispatcher.maybeSpeakInitialDirection on Glass; ride-service forwards OsmAnd's voice events as TTS payloads. Less code on both sides; phrasing quality is owned by OsmAnd rather than our composer.
close_reason: See notes for full analysis. 0ha: ship-with-AIDL + overlay tweaks; getBitmapForGpx viable, no Option C needed. 51h: Option A — pipe OsmAnd's params.played array straight to Glass TTS.
